task: Write a Python function title_words(s) returning the string with every whitespace-separated word title-cased (first letter upper, the rest lower) and runs of whitespace collapsed to single spaces, except that words already entirely uppercase stay exactly as they are. An empty string returns ''. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The code correctly implements the title-case functionality for words and collapses whitespace runs, handling edge cases as expected.
trace: The function `title_words` takes a string `s` as input. It splits the string into words using `split()` which automatically collapses multiple spaces into single spaces and handles leading/trailing spaces by returning an empty list for an empty or whitespace-only string. Then, it iterates over each word with a generator expression that title-cases all words except those that are already in uppercase, which remain unchanged. Finally, it joins the processed words back into a single string with `join()`, using a space as the separator. This approach ensures the function is concise and readable while correctly handling edge cases such as strings with leading/trailing spaces or multiple consecutive spaces.
